task_completion.description: Verify that Screaming Frog SEO Spider is open and the target URL has been crawled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog application window is visible with 'https://books.toscrape.com/' in the URL bar at the top, and the crawl progress bar indicates the crawl is active or completed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has navigated to the Internal HTML data within Screaming Frog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Internal' tab is selected in Screaming Frog, the filter is set to 'HTML', and the data table is visible showing columns, specifically including 'Text to Code Ratio'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent exports the internal HTML data to the specified CSV file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing the file 'code_bloat_data.csv' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/') directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent creates the text report file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager showing the file 'bloat_analysis.txt' being created or existing in the '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/') directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the text report contains the top 5 pages with the lowest text-to-code ratios and the approximate average ratio for the site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'bloat_analysis.txt' are visible in a text editor, explicitly listing 5 URLs/pages with their corresponding text-to-code ratios and a stated average text-to-code ratio for the entire site.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Screaming Frog SEO Spider GUI to crawl the site.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog GUI with the crawl data populated. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like curl or wget) or custom Python scripts to bypass the GUI tool.

integrity.description: Ensure the CSV was exported via the Screaming Frog GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export action should originate from the Screaming Frog 'Export' button or menu, rather than a terminal command generating a fake CSV.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the actual crawl data to write the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen viewing the sorted 'Text to Code Ratio' column in Screaming Frog or opening the exported CSV in a spreadsheet/text viewer before or while writing the report, proving they didn't hallucinate the values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL to crawl: https://books.toscrape.com/ (Domain: books.toscrape.com). The exported CSV must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/code_bloat_data.csv and must contain a 'Text to Code Ratio' column with at least 20 rows of URL data. The text report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/bloat_analysis.txt.
